text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Egg yolks and whole eggs contain significant amounts of protein and choline.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Egg yolks and whole eggs contain significant amounts of eggs come from yolk fat.","entailment":"The source quantifies the proportion of egg calories derived from yolk fat.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"calorie source proportion","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"more than half","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"A 50-gram egg contains roughly five grams of fat, with saturated fat comprising 33 percent, monounsaturated fat 38 percent, polyunsaturated fat 20 percent, and cholesterol 4 percent.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 50-gram egg contains roughly five grams of fat, with saturated fat comprising 33 percent, monounsaturated fat 38 percent, polyunsaturated fat 20 percent, and cholesterol 4 percent.","entailment":"The source provides detailed composition percentages for a 50‑gram listed","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The egg white consists primarily of water and protein with minimal cholesterol and fat.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The egg white consists primarily of water and protein with minimal cholesterol and fat.","entailment":"The source describes the main components of egg white, emphasizing low cholesterol and fat.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"egg white composition","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Some research indicates dietary cholesterol raises the total-to-HDL cholesterol ratio negatively.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Some research indicates dietary cholesterol raises the total-to-HDL cholesterol ratio negatively","entailment":"The source reports that certain studies found a causal effect of dietary cholesterol on worsening the total‑to‑HDL ratio.","population":"","intervention":"dietary people","intervention":"consuming up to one egg daily","comparator":"","outcome":"heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to one egg daily","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"One systematic review found that higher egg consumption correlated with reduced coronary artery disease risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"One systematic review found that higher egg consumption correlated with reduced coronary artery disease risk.","entailment":"The source notes a systematic review that observed an association between greater egg intake and lower CAD risk.","population":"","intervention":"higher egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"coronary artery disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Another analysis found associations with increased all-cause mortality and cardiovascular disease separate analysis observed a link between egg consumption and higher mortality rates.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"all-cause mortality and cardiovascular disease mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A 2018 meta-analysis of randomized trials showed eggs increase total and LDL cholesterol consumption","entailment":"The source cites a 2018 meta‑analysis indicating a causal increase in total and LDL cholesterol when eggs are consumed versus none.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"no egg consumption","outcome":"total and LDL cholesterol","timeframe":"2018","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"More recent reviews from 2020 concluded that moderate daily egg consumption shows no increased cardiovascular disease risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"More recent reviews from 2020 concluded that moderate daily egg consumption shows no
